step_by_step_thinking:
The child presents with symptoms suggestive of pulpitis or an acute inflammatory response to carious lesions. Given that these teeth (61, 62) may have active pulpal involvement due to recurrent swelling and pain, endodontic therapy would aim to eliminate infection and relieve pain by accessing and cleaning the pulp chamber followed by obturation.

My answer: A: Endodontic treatment To address the recurrent inflammation and potential pulpal involvement, endodontic treatment provides direct access to treat any infected tissue within the tooth's root canal system. This approach can alleviate current discomfort and prevent future complications more effectively than other options provided for this age group. My
